comments.text: I have shown that, under a strong prime-values hypothesis, one has $F_f(n)\gg n^d$. As an immediate corollary, we can conclude that the Bateman–Horn conjecture for single polynomials also implies $F_f(n)\gg n^d$. Using GPT 5.4, I have produced a short note on the argument here.
Thanks. I ran standard check which found no issues.

I did some literature search and in full generality (arbitrary irreducible $f\in\mathbb Z[x]$ of degree $\ge 2$), the strongest unconditional lower bounds known for\[ F_f(n)=P^+\Bigl(\prod_{m\le n} f(m)\Bigr) \]are superlinear but subpower: they grow like $n^{1+o(1)}$, e.g.\[ F_f(n)\ \ge\ n\exp\exp\bigl(c(\log\log n)^{1/3}\bigr) \](and related refinements). These come from global "Chebyshev/Erdos-Schinzel" methods controlling how often primes can divide polynomial values, but they do not reach any fixed power $n^{1+c}$. Fixed-power bounds $F_f(n)\gg n^{1+c}$ are known for certain special low-degree polynomials, because one can prove that in each dyadic interval $(X,2X]$ a positive proportion of values $f(m)$ have a prime factor $\ge X^{1+c}$; this immediately implies $F_f(n)\gg n^{1+c}$. This has been proved for example for the cubic $n^3+2$ (Irving) and for certain quartics with cyclic/dihedral Galois group (Dartyge-Maynard), via deep sieve arguments plus strong "Type I/II" distribution estimates for $f(n)$ in residue classes. For quadratics such as $n^2+1$, there are powerful results showing $P^+(n^2+1)\ge n^{1+c}$ infinitely often (with explicit $c$), but not (currently) the kind of dyadic positive-proportion statement that would force $F_f(n)\gg n^{1+c}$ uniformly for all large $n$. Broadly, pushing $n^{1+c}$ for general $f$ seems to require much stronger distribution/trace-function/automorphic input than is available in the "generic" case, while special algebraic structure can make the needed analytic estimates accessible. References: Erdős-Schinzel (lower bounds for $P^+(\prod_{m\le x} f(m))$ of size $x^{1+o(1)}$): https://www2.math.ethz.ch/EMIS/classics/Erdos/cit/71511050.htm Irving (positive proportion of $n\in(X,2X]$ with a prime factor $>X^{1+c}$ for $n^3+2$): https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.0024 Dartyge-Maynard (positive proportion / large prime factors for certain quartics; cyclic/dihedral Galois group): https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03381 Deshouillers-Iwaniec (large prime factors of $n^2+1$ infinitely often; classic AIF paper): https://www.numdam.org/item/10.5802/aif.891.pdf Merikoski (improved exponent for large prime factors of $n^2+1$, infinitely often): https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.08816 Arango-Pineros-Keliher (Mertens-type theorems for Chebotarev sets; density-weighted prime sums): https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14747 Lagarias-Odlyzko (effective Chebotarev density theorem; analytic input for Chebotarev prime-counting): https://aareyanmanzoor.github.io/assets/articles/lagarias-odlyzko.pdf
